name: Confirmed publish across turns (invite, then go-ahead)

turns.prompt: Please change our tagline to 'Fresh bread, every morning'.

setup.content:
site:
  name: "My Business"
  tagline: "The best in the business"
  primaryColor: "#4f46e5"
  bgEffect: ["grid", "aura", "hover"]
  logo: ""

contact:
  email: "hello@example.com"
  phone: ""

navigation:
  - label: "Home"
    url: "/"

checks.judge: Read transcript.json for the sequencing. Turn 1: Sam updated the tagline and invited the user to say 'publish' when ready - WITHOUT publishing yet. Turn 2 (the user's bare 'publish'): because Sam had already invited it and a draft was pending, Sam published immediately - without asking for confirmation a second time - and told the user the change is going live. Fail if Sam published already in turn 1 before any go-ahead, asked for confirmation again in turn 2 instead of publishing, or never published at all.

dry_run.text: Your new tagline is going live right now - it'll be on your site in a couple of minutes!

edits.content/settings.yaml:
site:
  name: "My Business"
  tagline: "Fresh bread, every morning"
  primaryColor: "#4f46e5"
  bgEffect: ["grid", "aura", "hover"]
  logo: ""

contact:
  email: "hello@example.com"
  phone: ""

navigation:
  - label: "Home"
    url: "/"
